back: The company (founded 2013 by Spark's creators) and managed cloud platform that makes Spark easy to use.
A Spark cluster that Databricks provisions, configures, and maintains for you — created with a few clicks.
Clusters that add or remove machines with demand and shut off when idle, controlling cost automatically.
A collaborative document where you write Python/SQL/Scala/R in cells and see results inline, editing with teammates in real time.
An optimized version of Spark with built-in performance, reliability, and security improvements.
A high-speed query engine inside the Databricks Runtime that accelerates SQL and DataFrame work.
Compute with no clusters to manage: Databricks instantly supplies and scales it, and you pay only for what you use.
Scheduled, automated work that runs tasks in order — on a schedule or trigger — with retries and alerts.
Configuration files that define jobs and pipelines as version-controlled code, enabling reliable CI/CD deployment.
Databricks offered as a first-party service on Microsoft's Azure cloud, integrated with Azure storage and security.
